# CONSTITUTION — Knowledge-Hub
# Generated by /constitution create. Edit via /constitution update only.
# Version: 1.2 | Date: 2026-03-12

---

## Purpose
Brysen Group internal AI knowledge platform.
Enable all employees to instantly access company knowledge across languages and countries.
API-first: all interfaces (Web, Teams, Slack) consume the same /v1/* endpoints.

---

## Principles

| ID   | Principle | Rationale |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| P001 | Spec before code. No implementation without a task file. | Prevents drift, enables async team collaboration |
| P002 | RBAC is foundational, not an afterthought. Applied at DB layer. | Data leakage across orgs is unacceptable |
| P003 | Multilingual by design. Japanese, English, Vietnamese, Korean treated equally. | No language is a second-class citizen |
| P004 | API contract is the source of truth for all consumers. | Bots and SPA must behave identically |
| P005 | Fail fast, fail visibly. Structured errors, no silent failures. | Debugging across languages and timezones requires clarity |
| P006 | Performance is a feature. Latency SLA is non-negotiable. | Slow search means unused platform |
| P007 | Memory is layered and intentional. Load only what the task requires. | Token budget discipline keeps agents fast and focused |
| P008 | Every agent owns its boundary. Cross-scope calls via interfaces only. | Prevents tight coupling and accidental data access |

---

## Hard Constraints

| ID   | Constraint |
|------|------------|
| C001 | RBAC filter applied at pgvector WHERE clause — never post-retrieval in Python |
| C002 | PII must never appear in vector metadata — doc_id, lang, user_group_id, created_at only |
| C003 | All /v1/* endpoints require authentication. /v1/health is the sole exception |
| C004 | API versioning: /v1/ prefix mandatory. Breaking changes require /v2/ |
| C005 | CJK languages (ja, ko, zh) require language-aware tokenizer (MeCab for ja, KoNLPy/Mecab-ko for ko, jieba for zh). Whitespace split is forbidden |
| C006 | Vietnamese (vi) requires underthesea tokenizer before BM25 indexing |
| C007 | Hybrid search weights (BM25=0.3, Dense=0.7) are configurable via env vars, never hardcoded |
| C008 | Audit log required for every document retrieval: user_id, doc_id, timestamp, query_hash |
| C009 | Query language must be auto-detected — never hardcode lang="en" as fallback |
| C010 | All schema changes via numbered migration files. ORM models updated after migration review |
| C011 | PostgreSQL connection pool: min=5, max=20. No per-request connection creation |
| C012 | Embedding API calls must be batched (min 32 docs). No per-document loop calls |
| C013 | Rate limiting enforced: /v1/query 60 req/min, /v1/documents 20 req/min per user |
| C014 | AI answers must cite ≥1 source document. No answer generated if no relevant chunks found. Confidence < 0.4 triggers low-confidence warning |

---

## Non-Negotiables
These cannot be overridden by any spec, plan, or task:

- Zero PII in vector store
- Zero hardcoded secrets in source code
- Zero anonymous access to document endpoints
- Zero breaking changes to /v1/ without a version bump to /v2/
- Zero SQL string interpolation — parameterized queries only
- Zero implementation without a spec and task file
- Zero cross-agent imports outside defined interface boundaries

---

## Tech Stack (locked)

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| Backend | FastAPI (Python) |
| RAG | Hybrid search: multilingual dense embeddings + BM25 + RBAC hard-filter + conflict detection |
| Storage | PostgreSQL + pgvector (HNSW index required) + CJK-aware full-text search |
| Auth | API-key for bots/service accounts; OIDC/SSO Bearer for human users |
| Frontend | React/Vite SPA (thin client — no business logic) |
| Bots | Teams adapter + Slack adapter (consume /v1/* same as SPA) |

Changes to any of the above require a constitution update and team sign-off.

---

## Team Conventions

### Naming
- Files: `snake_case.py`, migrations: `NNN_description.sql`
- Classes: `PascalCase`, constants: `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`
- API routes: `/v1/<resource>` (plural nouns)
- Feature branches: `feature/<feature-name>`

### PR Rules
- Every PR references a task ID: `T00X: <title>`
- Every PR has passing tests before merge
- Security-touching PRs require `/reviewcode --level security` output attached

### Testing
- Backend unit test coverage ≥ 80% for new code
- Integration tests required for all critical user journeys
- AI answer quality validated via golden dataset regression suite

### Language in Code
- Code, comments, variable names: English only
- User-facing strings: localized via i18n layer, never hardcoded in source

### SDD Flow (mandatory order)
```
/constitution → /specify → /clarify → /checklist
     → /plan → /tasks → /analyze → /implement → /reviewcode
          ↓
     → /report
```
